sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a man (see below אֵנֶשׁ ), i.q. אָדָם, but only in poetic language. It is rarely used as a singular in sense, Psa 55:14 Job 5:17 generally coll. of the whole human race, Job 7:17 15:14 Psal. 8:5 . [This latter passage applies to Christ solely; see Heb 2:6.] The same is בֶּן־אֱנֹושׁ Psa 144:3. Specially it is-(a) a multitude, the common people; hence Isa 8:1, בְּחֶרֶט אֱנֹושׁ “with common writing,” i.e. with common letters, not those artificially formed, so as to be easily read by the illiterate. Comp. my Comm. on the passage, Rev 13:18 21:17 , and κατὰ ἄνθρωπον, Gal 3:15. [This is no illustration at all, the apostle opposes God to man].-(b) wicked men, Psa 9:20 56:2 66:12 . Comp. אָדָם No. 1.

(2) pr.n., [Enos], Enosh, the son of Seth, the grandson of Adam, Gen 4:26 5:6 , .
